name: Ruth Almans Sourdough Starter
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Yeast (use yeast only to speed action)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the potato water, sugar, flour, and yeast in a sourdough pot.
2. Boil potatoes with jackets on until they fall apart.
3. Remove the skins, mash the potatoes to create a puree, and let cool.
4. If necessary, add more water to achieve sufficient liquid consistency. A richer starter will result from using more ingredients.
5. Mix all ingredients in the pot until smooth and creamy.
6. Cover and set aside to begin fermentation.
My Note: After about 3 days of active growth, transfer the starter to the refrigerator. For each use, take out an equal amount of flour and water as needed for bread or pancakes. One teaspoon will yield a phenomenal amount of starter – this is truly a killer sourdough.
